title: GeometricConstraints Object
description: A collection of all of the geometric constraints in a sketch. This object also supports the methods to create new geometric constraints.

methods.name: addCircularPattern
methods.description: Creates a new circular pattern in the sketch.

methods.name: addCoincident
methods.description: Creates a new coincident constraint between two entities. The first argument is a sketch point. The second argument is a sketch curve or point.

methods.name: addCoincidentToSurface
methods.description: Creates a new coincident constraint between the sketch point and surface. This forces the point to lie on the surface.

methods.name: addCollinear
methods.description: Creates a new collinear constraint between two lines.

methods.name: addConcentric
methods.description: Creates a new concentric constraint between two circles, arcs, ellipses, or elliptical arcs.

methods.name: addEqual
methods.description: Creates a new equal constraint between two lines, or between arcs and circles.

methods.name: addHorizontal
methods.description: Creates a new horizontal constraint on a line.

methods.name: addHorizontalPoints
methods.description: Creates a new horizontal constraint between two points.

methods.name: addLineOnPlanarSurface
methods.description: Creates a new constraint that forces the sketch line to lie on a planar surface.

methods.name: addLineParallelToPlanarSurface
methods.description: Creates a new parallel constraint between a sketch line and a planar surface that will constrain the line to lie on a plane parallel to the specified surface.

methods.name: addMidPoint
methods.description: Creates a new midpoint constraint between a point and a curve.

methods.name: addOffset
methods.description: **RETIRED** Creates an offset constraint, which results in creating a new set of curves that are an offset of the input curves. The returned offset constraint provides access to the created curves and the parameter controlling the offset.   The offset direction is implied by the flow direction of the provided curves. For example, if two connected lines are offset, the flow direction is from line 1 to line 2. A positive offset value creates the offset curve to the "right" of the lines and a negative offset goes to the "left". Left and right are evaluated with respect to the geometry. If you are standing on line 1 looking towards line 2 your left and right are the offset left and right. For closed single curves like circles and ellipses their flow direction is always counterclockwise so a positive offset is always to the outsides.

methods.name: addOffset2
methods.description: Creates an offset constraint, which results in creating a new set of curves that are an offset of the input curves. The returned offset constraint object provides access to the created curves and the parameter controlling the offset.

methods.name: addParallel
methods.description: Creates a new parallel constraint between two lines.

methods.name: addPerpendicular
methods.description: Creates a new perpendicular constraint between two lines.

methods.name: addPerpendicularToSurface
methods.description: Creates a new perpendicular constraint that forces the sketch curve to be perpendicular to the specified surface. Line and spline curves are supported.

methods.name: addPolygon
methods.description: Creates a polygon constraint on existing lines in the sketch.

methods.name: addRectangularPattern
methods.description: Creates a new rectangular pattern in the sketch.

methods.name: addSmooth
methods.description: Creates a new smooth constraint between two curves. One of the curves must be a spline. The other curve can be a spline or any other type of curve.

methods.name: addSymmetry
methods.description: Creates a new symmetry constraint.

methods.name: addTangent
methods.description: Creates a new tangent constraint between two curves.

methods.name: addTwoSidesOffset
methods.description: Creates two offset constraints, which results in creating two new sets of curves that are an offset of the input curves on each side of the original set of curves. The returned offset constraint objects provide access to the created curves and the parameters controlling the offsets.

methods.name: addVertical
methods.description: Creates a new vertical constraint on a line.

methods.name: addVerticalPoints
methods.description: Creates a new vertical constraint between two points.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createCircularPatternInput
methods.description: Creates a CircularPatternConstraintInput object. Use properties and methods on this object to define the circular pattern you want to create and then use the Add method, passing in the CircularPatternConstraintInput object.

methods.name: createOffsetInput
methods.description: Creates an OffsetConstraintInput object. Use properties and methods on this object to define the offset you want to create and then use the addOffset2 method, passing in the OffsetConstraintInput object, to create the offset.

methods.name: createRectangularPatternInput
methods.description: Creates a new RectangularPatternConstraintInput object. Use this object to define the various settings associated with a rectangular pattern in a sketch. Once the pattern is defined you can call the addRectangularPattern method and pass in the input object to create the sketch rectangular pattern.

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified sketch constraint using an index into the collection.

properties.name: count
properties.description: Returns the number of constraints in the sketch.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: API Sample that demonstrates creating sketch lines in various ways.
related_samples.description: Demonstrates several ways to create sketch lines, including as the result of creating a rectangle.

addCircularPattern.title: GeometricConstraints.addCircularPattern Method
addCircularPattern.description: Creates a new circular pattern in the sketch.
addCircularPattern.return_description: Returns the newly created CircularPatternConstraint object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: A CircularPatternConstraintInput object that defines the desired circular pattern. Use the createCircularPatternInput method to create a new CircularPatternConstraintInput object and then use methods on it to define the circular pattern.

addCoincident.title: GeometricConstraints.addCoincident Method
addCoincident.description: Creates a new coincident constraint between two entities. The first argument is a sketch point. The second argument is a sketch curve or point.
addCoincident.return_description: Returns the newly created CoincidentConstraint object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: point
parameters.description: The SketchPoint that will be made coincident.

parameters.name: entity
parameters.description: The SketchPoint or sketch curve that the point will be made coincident to.

addCoincidentToSurface.title: GeometricConstraints.addCoincidentToSurface Method
addCoincidentToSurface.description: Creates a new coincident constraint between the sketch point and surface. This forces the point to lie on the surface.
addCoincidentToSurface.return_description: Returns the newly created CoincidentToSurfaceConstraint object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: point
parameters.description: The SketchPoint to constrain to the surface.

parameters.name: surface
parameters.description: The BRepFace or ConstructionPlane the point will be coincident to.

addCollinear.title: GeometricConstraints.addCollinear Method
addCollinear.description: Creates a new collinear constraint between two lines.
addCollinear.return_description: Returns the newly created CollinearConstraint object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: lineOne
parameters.description: The first line to create the constraint on.

parameters.name: lineTwo
parameters.description: The second line to create the constraint on.

addConcentric.title: GeometricConstraints.addConcentric Method
addConcentric.description: Creates a new concentric constraint between two circles, arcs, ellipses, or elliptical arcs.
addConcentric.return_description: Returns the newly created ConcentricConstraint object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: entityOne
parameters.description: The first circle, arc, ellipse or elliptical arc.

parameters.name: entityTwo
parameters.description: The second circle, arc, ellipse or elliptical arc.

addEqual.title: GeometricConstraints.addEqual Method
addEqual.description: Creates a new equal constraint between two lines, or between arcs and circles.
addEqual.return_description: Returns the newly created EqualConstraint object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: curveOne
parameters.description: The first line, arc, or circle.

parameters.name: curveTwo
parameters.description: The second line, arc, or circle.

addHorizontal.title: GeometricConstraints.addHorizontal Method
addHorizontal.description: Creates a new horizontal constraint on a line.
addHorizontal.return_description: Returns the newly created HorizontalConstraint object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: line
parameters.description: The line to constrain horizontally.

related_samples.name: API Sample that demonstrates creating sketch lines in various ways.
related_samples.description: Demonstrates several ways to create sketch lines, including as the result of creating a rectangle.

addHorizontalPoints.title: GeometricConstraints.addHorizontalPoints Method
addHorizontalPoints.description: Creates a new horizontal constraint between two points.
addHorizontalPoints.return_description: Returns the newly created HorizontalPointsConstraint object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: pointOne
parameters.description: The first SketchPoint to constrain horizontally.

parameters.name: pointTwo
parameters.description: The second SketchPoint to constrain horizontally.

addLineOnPlanarSurface.title: GeometricConstraints.addLineOnPlanarSurface Method
addLineOnPlanarSurface.description: Creates a new constraint that forces the sketch line to lie on a planar surface.
addLineOnPlanarSurface.return_description: Returns the newly created LineOnPlanarSurfaceConstraint object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: line
parameters.description: The SketchLine to constrain to the surface.

parameters.name: planarSurface
parameters.description: The planar BRepFace or CosntructionPlane the sketch line will lie on.

addLineParallelToPlanarSurface.title: GeometricConstraints.addLineParallelToPlanarSurface Method
addLineParallelToPlanarSurface.description: Creates a new parallel constraint between a sketch line and a planar surface that will constrain the line to lie on a plane parallel to the specified surface.
addLineParallelToPlanarSurface.return_description: Returns the newly created LineParallelToPlanarSurfaceConstraint object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: line
parameters.description: The SketchLine to constrain to the surface.

parameters.name: planarSurface
parameters.description: The planar BRepFace or CosntructionPlane the sketch line will be parallel to.

addMidPoint.title: GeometricConstraints.addMidPoint Method
addMidPoint.description: Creates a new midpoint constraint between a point and a curve.
addMidPoint.return_description: Returns the newly created MidPointConstraint object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: point
parameters.description: The point to constrain to the midpoint of a curve.

parameters.name: midPointCurve
parameters.description: The curve that defines the midpoint to constraint to.

addOffset.title: GeometricConstraints.addOffset Method
addOffset.description: This function is retired. See more information in the 'Remarks' section below. Creates an offset constraint, which results in creating a new set of curves that are an offset of the input curves. The returned offset constraint provides access to the created curves and the parameter controlling the offset. The offset direction is implied by the flow direction of the provided curves. For example, if two connected lines are offset, the flow direction is from line 1 to line 2. A positive offset value creates the offset curve to the "right" of the lines and a negative offset goes to the "left". Left and right are evaluated with respect to the geometry. If you are standing on line 1 looking towards line 2 your left and right are the offset left and right. For closed single curves like circles and ellipses their flow direction is always counterclockwise so a positive offset is always to the outsides. Remarks
addOffset.return_description: The created OffsetConstraint. You can use properties on the constraint to get information about the offset.

parameters.name: curves
parameters.description: A set of end connected curves. The Sketch.FindConnectedCurves method is a convenient way to get this set of curves.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The value that defines the offset. This is a ValueInput object so it can be a float value to define the offset in centimeters or it can be a string defining an expression that will be used by the dimension that controls the offset.

parameters.name: basePoint
parameters.description: The location on one of the curves where the offset dimension will be created.

addOffset2.title: GeometricConstraints.addOffset2 Method
addOffset2.description: Creates an offset constraint, which results in creating a new set of curves that are an offset of the input curves. The returned offset constraint object provides access to the created curves and the parameter controlling the offset.
addOffset2.return_description: Returns the newly created OffsetConstraint object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: The OffsetConstraintInput object that defines the offset to create.

addParallel.title: GeometricConstraints.addParallel Method
addParallel.description: Creates a new parallel constraint between two lines.
addParallel.return_description: Returns the newly created ParallelConstraint object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: lineOne
parameters.description: The first SketchLine.

parameters.name: lineTwo
parameters.description: The second SketchLine.

addPerpendicular.title: GeometricConstraints.addPerpendicular Method
addPerpendicular.description: Creates a new perpendicular constraint between two lines.
addPerpendicular.return_description: Returns the newly created PerpendicularConstraint object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: lineOne
parameters.description: The first SketchLine.

parameters.name: lineTwo
parameters.description: The second SketchLine.

addPerpendicularToSurface.title: GeometricConstraints.addPerpendicularToSurface Method
addPerpendicularToSurface.description: Creates a new perpendicular constraint that forces the sketch curve to be perpendicular to the specified surface. Line and spline curves are supported.
addPerpendicularToSurface.return_description: Returns the newly created PerpendicularToSurfaceConstraint object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: curve
parameters.description: The SketchCurve to constrain to the surface.

parameters.name: surface
parameters.description: The BRepFace or ConstructionPlane the line will be perpendicular to.

addPolygon.title: GeometricConstraints.addPolygon Method
addPolygon.description: Creates a polygon constraint on existing lines in the sketch.
addPolygon.return_description: Returns the created PolygonConstraint or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: lines
parameters.description: An array of existing SketchLine objects in this sketch that define a valid polygon. They must be the same length, connect at their endpoints, have the same angle between them, and define a closed shape. The order of the lines within the array does not matter.

addRectangularPattern.title: GeometricConstraints.addRectangularPattern Method
addRectangularPattern.description: Creates a new rectangular pattern in the sketch.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: A RectangularPatternConstraintInput object that defines all of inputs required to create the desired rectangular pattern. Use the createRectangularPatternInput method to create the input object.

addSmooth.title: GeometricConstraints.addSmooth Method
addSmooth.description: Creates a new smooth constraint between two curves. One of the curves must be a spline. The other curve can be a spline or any other type of curve.
addSmooth.return_description: Returns the newly created SmoothConstraint object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: curveOne
parameters.description: The first curve to be constrained to be smooth to the second curve.

parameters.name: curveTwo
parameters.description: The second curve to be constrained to be smooth to the first curve.

addSymmetry.title: GeometricConstraints.addSymmetry Method
addSymmetry.return_description: Returns the newly created SymmetryConstraint object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: entityOne
parameters.description: The first sketch entity to be symmetric.

parameters.name: entityTwo
parameters.description: The second sketch entity to be symmetric. It must be the same type as the first entity.

parameters.name: symmetryLine
parameters.description: The SketchLine that defines the axis of symmetry.

addTangent.title: GeometricConstraints.addTangent Method
addTangent.description: Creates a new tangent constraint between two curves.
addTangent.return_description: Returns the newly created TangentConstraint object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: curveOne
parameters.description: The first curve to be tangent.

parameters.name: curveTwo
parameters.description: The second curve to be tangent.

related_samples.name: Create Circle By 3 Tangents API Sample
related_samples.description: Creates three lines and then draws a circle that is tangent to the lines. It then creates tangent constraints to maintain that relationship.

addTwoSidesOffset.title: GeometricConstraints.addTwoSidesOffset Method
addTwoSidesOffset.description: Creates two offset constraints, which results in creating two new sets of curves that are an offset of the input curves on each side of the original set of curves. The returned offset constraint objects provide access to the created curves and the parameters controlling the offsets.
addTwoSidesOffset.return_description: Returns an array containing the two OffsetConstraint objects or errors if the creation failed.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: The OffsetConstraintInput object that defines the offset to create. The same definition applies to both offsets that are created.

parameters.name: linkOffsets
parameters.description: Defines if the parameter driving the offset of the second side should reference the parameter of the first side. This sets up the parameters so if the first side is edited, the second side will automatically update using the same value. A value of true will create the linked parameter.

addVertical.title: GeometricConstraints.addVertical Method
addVertical.description: Creates a new vertical constraint on a line.
addVertical.return_description: Returns the newly created VerticalConstraint object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: line
parameters.description: The line to constrain vertically.

related_samples.name: API Sample that demonstrates creating sketch lines in various ways.
related_samples.description: Demonstrates several ways to create sketch lines, including as the result of creating a rectangle.

addVerticalPoints.title: GeometricConstraints.addVerticalPoints Method
addVerticalPoints.description: Creates a new vertical constraint between two points.
addVerticalPoints.return_description: Returns the newly created VerticalPointsConstraint object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: pointOne
parameters.description: The first SketchPoint to constrain vertically.

parameters.name: pointTwo
parameters.description: The second SketchPoint to constrain vertically.

classType.title: GeometricConstraints.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createCircularPatternInput.title: GeometricConstraints.createCircularPatternInput Method
createCircularPatternInput.description: Creates a CircularPatternConstraintInput object. Use properties and methods on this object to define the circular pattern you want to create and then use the Add method, passing in the CircularPatternConstraintInput object.
createCircularPatternInput.return_description: Returns the newly created CircularPatternConstraintInput object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: inputEntities
parameters.description: An array of sketch entities to be patterned. All of the entities must be from the current sketch.

parameters.name: centerPoint
parameters.description: A SketchPoint from the same sketch that defines the center of the pattern.

createOffsetInput.title: GeometricConstraints.createOffsetInput Method
createOffsetInput.description: Creates an OffsetConstraintInput object. Use properties and methods on this object to define the offset you want to create and then use the addOffset2 method, passing in the OffsetConstraintInput object, to create the offset.
createOffsetInput.return_description: Returns an OffsetConstraintInput object or null in the case of invalid input.

parameters.name: curves
parameters.description: A set of end connected curves. The Sketch.FindConnectedCurves method is a convenient way to get this set of curves.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: The value that defines the offset. This is a ValueInput object so it can be a float value to define the offset in centimeters or it can be a string defining an expression that will be used by the parameter controlling the offset. A positive offset value creates the offset curve to the "right" and a negative offset value goes to the "left".   The flow direction of the provided curves implies the offset direction. For example, if two connected lines are offset, the flow direction is from line 1 to line 2. Left and right are evaluated relative to the input geometry. If you are standing on line 1 and looking towards line 2, the offset's left side is on your left, and the right side is to your right. Closed single curves like circles and ellipses always have a counterclockwise flow, so a positive offset is always to the outside. For closed splines, the positive direction is based on the spline's parameterization.

createRectangularPatternInput.title: GeometricConstraints.createRectangularPatternInput Method
createRectangularPatternInput.description: Creates a new RectangularPatternConstraintInput object. Use this object to define the various settings associated with a rectangular pattern in a sketch. Once the pattern is defined you can call the addRectangularPattern method and pass in the input object to create the sketch rectangular pattern.
createRectangularPatternInput.return_description: Returns the created RectangularPatternsConstraintInput object or null in the case of failure.

parameters.name: entities
parameters.description: An array of sketch entities to pattern. These can be sketch points and curves.

parameters.name: distanceType
parameters.description: Specifies if the distances defined for the pattern define the overall size of the pattern or the distance between the rows and columns.

item.title: GeometricConstraints.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified sketch constraint using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

count.title: GeometricConstraints.count Property
count.description: Returns the number of constraints in the sketch.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = geometricConstraints_var.count

isValid.title: GeometricConstraints.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = geometricConstraints_var.isValid

objectType.title: GeometricConstraints.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = geometricConstraints_var.objectType
